coverage.notes: The MVP wireframe set now covers the Home/Landing route, all required public routes, all required admin routes, and support screens for login, password reset, 404, 500, offline/maintenance, media upload, article editor workflow, and mobile-only comps.

route_separation.notes: Each distinct public or admin screen is delivered as its own HTML file. mvp-wireframes.html provides a route overview without replacing the existing index.html home wireframe.

visual_consistency.notes: New screens use the same grayscale redline system, B&B mark, browser frame, mono labels, numbered pins, and right-side annotation panel as the existing home artifact.

public_editorial_ia.notes: Public screens preserve editorial magazine structure: mission/vision, profile directories, published article metadata, category/search behavior, media credits/alt text, article body rhythm, related articles, and contact flow.

admin_operational_density.notes: Admin screens are dense and operational, using tables, filters, metrics, queues, detail panels, editor forms, upload/select placeholders, and moderation actions rather than marketing-style cards.

states_and_accessibility.notes: Every route includes state notes for loading, empty/no-results, error, validation, success, permission, or moderation transitions where relevant. Pin controls use native buttons, labelled aria-controls, aria-pressed, focus-visible styling, and shared keyboard behavior.

anti_ai_feel.notes: No decorative gradients, glows, stock imagery, invented metrics, or generic SaaS feature stacks were added. Greyboxes remain practical and content-specific to Babas & Brasse.

implementation_readiness.notes: Shared CSS and JS keep screens consistent while each file maps cleanly to an implementation route or support flow. Spec panels call out responsive behavior, public/admin boundaries, approval rules, SEO handoff, auth recovery, error recovery, form validation, media metadata, and mobile implementation checks.
